learning_objectives: Understand what MCP is and why it exists
Identify the three main MCP primitives (tools, resources, prompts)
Run a simple FastMCP server with one tool

ordered_steps.title: Read the MCP overview
ordered_steps.description: Read the official MCP introduction page and the FastMCP README. Focus on: what problem MCP solves, the host/client/server architecture.

ordered_steps.title: Install FastMCP
ordered_steps.description: Run: pip install fastmcp. Verify with: python -c 'import fastmcp; print(fastmcp.__version__)'

ordered_steps.title: Run the hello-world MCP server
ordered_steps.description: Copy the minimal FastMCP example with one tool (greet). Start the server with mcp.run(transport='http'). Verify it starts without errors.

ordered_steps.title: Call the tool from a client
ordered_steps.description: Use fastmcp.Client to connect and call your greet tool. Print the result.

ordered_steps.title: Reflect
ordered_steps.description: Write one sentence explaining MCP to a colleague who has never heard of it.

practice_suggestions: Add a second tool that returns the current time
Try calling list_tools() from the client and print all available tools

learning_objectives: Understand the full MCP architecture (host, client, server)
Build a multi-tool FastMCP server with realistic tools
Connect an LLM to MCP tools via LangChain

ordered_steps.title: Architecture deep-dive
ordered_steps.description: Study the MCP spec: host, client, server, tools, resources, transport types. Draw the architecture on paper.

ordered_steps.title: Build a 3-tool MCP server
ordered_steps.description: Create a FastMCP server with: get_weather(city), get_time(timezone), greet(name). Run it on localhost:8003.

ordered_steps.title: Explore tools with the MCP client
ordered_steps.description: Write a client script that: pings the server, lists all tools with their schemas, calls each tool.

ordered_steps.title: Connect LangChain to MCP
ordered_steps.description: Use AzureChatOpenAI with bind_tools() to let the LLM call your MCP tools. Ask: 'What is the time in London?'

ordered_steps.title: Add error handling
ordered_steps.description: Make your tools return structured error dicts for invalid inputs instead of raising exceptions.

ordered_steps.title: Review and document
ordered_steps.description: Add docstrings to all tools. These become the tool descriptions the LLM sees.

practice_suggestions: Add a 4th tool: get_joke(category) that returns a hardcoded joke
Observe how the LLM decides which tool to call based on tool descriptions

learning_objectives: Master the full MCP specification for beginners
Build a production-style MCP server with tags, data files, and structured responses
Understand the agentic loop with MCP tools
Write tests for MCP tools

ordered_steps.title: MCP specification study
ordered_steps.description: Read the full MCP spec. Focus on primitives, lifecycle, error types, and the difference between tools, resources, and prompts.

ordered_steps.title: Build a realistic MCP server
ordered_steps.description: Create a server with 5 tools that read from local JSON files. Include tool tags, proper docstrings, and structured error responses.

ordered_steps.title: Build the agentic loop
ordered_steps.description: Create a Python script where an LLM has access to all tools and can chain them to answer multi-step questions.

ordered_steps.title: Write MCP tool tests
ordered_steps.description: Write tests for each tool: happy path, error path, boundary conditions. Use pytest-asyncio.

ordered_steps.title: Documentation and reflection
ordered_steps.description: Document each tool in a README. Write a reflection on what you would do differently in production.

practice_suggestions: Add a tool that reads from a real API (e.g. a free weather API) and compare with your local JSON version
Implement tag filtering and test that agents only receive their relevant tools

learning_objectives: Implement tag-based tool filtering in an MCP client
Build a 2-step agentic loop

ordered_steps.title: Review tag filtering code
ordered_steps.description: Read the _get_mcp_tools_by_tags() pattern from the workshop reference implementation.

ordered_steps.title: Add tags to your existing tools
ordered_steps.description: Refactor an existing MCP server to use @mcp.tool(tags={'category'}) for all tools.

ordered_steps.title: Implement filtered tool access
ordered_steps.description: Write a client that only loads tools tagged 'data' and verify the LLM cannot call tools from other categories.

practice_suggestions: Create two agents that share one MCP server but access different tool subsets via tags

learning_objectives: Master tag-based tool filtering across multiple agent types
Build a full 5-iteration agentic loop with MCP
Handle tool call errors gracefully in the loop

ordered_steps.title: Design a multi-category MCP server
ordered_steps.description: Design and implement a server with tools in 3 tag categories: data, compute, and io. Each category has 2 tools.

ordered_steps.title: Build 3 specialist agents
ordered_steps.description: Create 3 agent logic classes, each filtering for only their tag category. Test each independently.

ordered_steps.title: Implement the full agentic loop
ordered_steps.description: Add the 5-iteration loop with tool_calls detection, call_tool execution, and result injection into the message history.

ordered_steps.title: Add error recovery
ordered_steps.description: If a tool call fails, inject a friendly error message into the history and let the LLM try a different approach.

practice_suggestions: Test what happens when the LLM is given a question that requires tools from two different categories

learning_objectives: Architect a shared MCP server for a complete multi-agent system
Implement all tag-filtered agents with full agentic loops
Write comprehensive integration tests
Handle all edge cases: unavailable server, unknown tools, loop overflow

ordered_steps.title: Architecture design
ordered_steps.description: Design the full system: which agents, which tools, which tag categories. Draw the flow diagram.

ordered_steps.title: Implement the MCP server
ordered_steps.description: Build the shared FastMCP server with all tools across all categories.

ordered_steps.title: Implement all agents
ordered_steps.description: Build each specialist agent with tag filtering and agentic loops.

ordered_steps.title: Integration tests
ordered_steps.description: Write tests that start all servers and verify end-to-end flows work correctly.

ordered_steps.title: Edge case hardening
ordered_steps.description: Test and fix: MCP server down, tool returns error, loop hits max iterations, unknown tool called.

ordered_steps.title: Performance profiling
ordered_steps.description: Measure latency per tool call and total response time. Identify the slowest step.

practice_suggestions: Add a circuit breaker: if the MCP server fails 3 times, disable tool use and fall back to LLM-only mode

learning_objectives: Review MCP resource management patterns
Sketch a production MCP deployment architecture

ordered_steps.title: Study MCP resources vs tools
ordered_steps.description: Read the MCP spec section on resources. Understand when to use a resource vs a tool.

ordered_steps.title: Design production deployment
ordered_steps.description: Sketch a production MCP architecture with: auth, logging, rate limiting, and horizontal scaling.

practice_suggestions: Add one MCP resource to an existing server and access it from a client

learning_objectives: Implement MCP sampling (server-initiated LLM calls)
Add structured observability to a production MCP server

ordered_steps.title: Study MCP sampling protocol
ordered_steps.description: Read the sampling section of the MCP spec. Understand the server→host request flow.

ordered_steps.title: Add structured logging
ordered_steps.description: Add JSON-structured logs to every tool call: tool_name, input_hash, output_size, latency_ms, error.

ordered_steps.title: Implement request tracing
ordered_steps.description: Propagate trace_id from A2A context through MCP tool calls and log it on both sides.

ordered_steps.title: Add rate limiting
ordered_steps.description: Implement per-client rate limiting on the MCP server using a token bucket algorithm.

practice_suggestions: Export tool call metrics to Prometheus and visualize in Grafana

learning_objectives: Build a production-grade MCP server with auth, tracing, rate limiting, and health checks
Implement a complete multi-agent system with the production MCP server
Perform load testing and optimization

ordered_steps.title: Production MCP server design
ordered_steps.description: Design the full production server with all cross-cutting concerns documented.

ordered_steps.title: Implement auth
ordered_steps.description: Add API key auth to the MCP server. Update all agents to include the key in requests.

ordered_steps.title: Add observability
ordered_steps.description: Structured logging, distributed tracing, metrics endpoint.

ordered_steps.title: Health checks
ordered_steps.description: Add /health and /ready endpoints. Include MCP server status in each agent's health check.

ordered_steps.title: Load testing
ordered_steps.description: Use locust or hey to load test the MCP server. Identify the throughput limit.

ordered_steps.title: Optimization and documentation
ordered_steps.description: Apply the top optimization from load testing. Document the production runbook.

practice_suggestions: Deploy the MCP server to a cloud VM and test latency from a remote A2A agent

learning_objectives: Understand the A2A protocol basics
Run a hello-world A2A agent

ordered_steps.title: Read A2A overview
ordered_steps.description: Read the A2A spec introduction and Agent Card format.

ordered_steps.title: Install A2A SDK
ordered_steps.description: pip install a2a-sdk. Review the SDK examples.

ordered_steps.title: Run a hello-world agent
ordered_steps.description: Start the simplest possible A2A agent and send a message to it.

practice_suggestions: Inspect the Agent Card JSON at /.well-known/agent-card.json

learning_objectives: Build a complete A2A agent with a skill, executor, and server
Send messages and receive streaming responses

ordered_steps.title: Study the agent anatomy
ordered_steps.description: Understand agent_card.py, agent_logic.py, agent_executor.py, __main__.py.

ordered_steps.title: Build your own agent
ordered_steps.description: Create a simple greeting agent with one skill and full A2A server setup.

ordered_steps.title: Build a client
ordered_steps.description: Write a Python client that sends a message and prints the streaming response.

ordered_steps.title: Add a second skill
ordered_steps.description: Add a second skill to your agent and verify routing via the Agent Card.

practice_suggestions: Add examples to your skill and see how they affect orchestrator routing

learning_objectives: Build a complete 2-agent system with orchestrator
Implement multi-turn conversation via context_id
Write agent tests

ordered_steps.title: System design
ordered_steps.description: Design a 2-specialist-agent system with an orchestrator.

ordered_steps.title: Build both agents
ordered_steps.description: Implement both specialist agents with realistic skills.

ordered_steps.title: Build the orchestrator
ordered_steps.description: Implement keyword routing and context_id forwarding.

ordered_steps.title: Multi-turn test
ordered_steps.description: Write a 4-turn conversation test that verifies context is preserved.

ordered_steps.title: Full test suite
ordered_steps.description: Write tests for all agents and the orchestrator.

practice_suggestions: Try breaking context_id forwarding and observe the behavior

learning_objectives: Review context_id forwarding and keyword routing algorithm

ordered_steps.title: Review orchestrator code
ordered_steps.description: Read the keyword routing logic in agent_logic.py. Trace through a sample routing decision.

ordered_steps.title: Test routing edge cases
ordered_steps.description: Send queries that should route to multiple agents. Verify the 50% threshold works.

practice_suggestions: Add a new skill to one agent and verify the orchestrator discovers it automatically

learning_objectives: Implement multi-agent orchestration with context_id
Add MCP tool integration to an A2A agent

ordered_steps.title: Add context_id forwarding
ordered_steps.description: Trace the context_id through orchestrator → remote agent and verify history is passed.

ordered_steps.title: Integrate MCP into an agent
ordered_steps.description: Add MCP tool calls to one of your agents using tag-filtered tool access.

ordered_steps.title: Write multi-turn tests
ordered_steps.description: Test a 3-turn conversation where follow-up questions use context from earlier turns.

practice_suggestions: Verify that removing history breaks multi-turn and re-adding it fixes it

learning_objectives: Build a complete 3-agent A2A + MCP system
Implement all 5 orchestrator flows
Full test coverage including multi-turn and MCP tests

ordered_steps.title: System architecture
ordered_steps.description: Design a 3-agent system with an MCP server. Define all skills and tools.

ordered_steps.title: Implement all agents
ordered_steps.description: Build all 3 specialist agents with MCP integration.

ordered_steps.title: Implement orchestrator flows
ordered_steps.description: Implement and test all 5 flows including multi-agent orchestration.

ordered_steps.title: Full test suite
ordered_steps.description: MCP tests, agent tests, orchestrator tests, memory tests.

practice_suggestions: Add a flow that requires all 3 agents to collaborate on a single response

learning_objectives: Review A2A task lifecycle and push notifications

ordered_steps.title: Study task lifecycle
ordered_steps.description: Map all task state transitions in the A2A spec to the TaskUpdater methods.

ordered_steps.title: Explore push notifications
ordered_steps.description: Read the A2A spec section on push notifications and webhooks.

practice_suggestions: Trigger INPUT_REQUIRED in a test and see how the client handles it

learning_objectives: Add agent authentication to a multi-agent system
Implement a dynamic agent registry

ordered_steps.title: Design auth flow
ordered_steps.description: Design API key auth for agent-to-agent calls.

ordered_steps.title: Implement auth
ordered_steps.description: Add auth headers to orchestrator calls and validation in remote agents.

ordered_steps.title: Dynamic registry
ordered_steps.description: Replace agents_registry.json with a simple HTTP registry that agents register with on startup.

practice_suggestions: Test what happens when an agent registers with an invalid agent card

learning_objectives: Build a production A2A system with auth, dynamic discovery, persistent task store, and observability

ordered_steps.title: Production design
ordered_steps.description: Design the full production system with all cross-cutting concerns.

ordered_steps.title: Persistent task store
ordered_steps.description: Replace InMemoryTaskStore with a Redis-backed implementation.

ordered_steps.title: Auth and dynamic discovery
ordered_steps.description: Implement full auth flow and service registry.

ordered_steps.title: Observability and health checks
ordered_steps.description: Add distributed tracing and health endpoints.

ordered_steps.title: Load test and document
ordered_steps.description: Load test the system and write the production runbook.

practice_suggestions: Deploy to Kubernetes and test rolling agent updates

learning_objectives: Build a minimal RAG pipeline from scratch

ordered_steps.title: Understand the concept
ordered_steps.description: Read the RAG overview. Draw the embed-retrieve-generate pipeline.

ordered_steps.title: Embed 5 documents
ordered_steps.description: Use sentence-transformers to embed 5 short paragraphs into vectors.

ordered_steps.title: Implement similarity search
ordered_steps.description: Compute cosine similarity between a query vector and all document vectors. Return top 2.

ordered_steps.title: Generate with context
ordered_steps.description: Inject the top 2 chunks into an LLM prompt and generate an answer.

practice_suggestions: Ask a question that cannot be answered without the retrieved context

learning_objectives: Build a complete RAG pipeline with FAISS and LangChain

ordered_steps.title: Install dependencies
ordered_steps.description: pip install faiss-cpu sentence-transformers langchain

ordered_steps.title: Chunk 3 documents
ordered_steps.description: Split 3 multi-paragraph documents into 200-token chunks.

ordered_steps.title: Build FAISS index
ordered_steps.description: Embed all chunks and build a FAISS index.

ordered_steps.title: Implement retrieval
ordered_steps.description: Write a retrieve(query, k=3) function.

ordered_steps.title: Build the RAG chain
ordered_steps.description: Connect retrieval to an LLM. Answer 5 test questions.

ordered_steps.title: Evaluate
ordered_steps.description: For each answer, manually score: Did it use the context correctly? Was the answer accurate?

practice_suggestions: Try k=1 vs k=5 and observe the difference in answer quality

learning_objectives: Build a production-quality beginner RAG system with chunking strategy, evaluation, and documentation

ordered_steps.title: Chunking strategy
ordered_steps.description: Implement and compare 3 chunking strategies: fixed-size, sentence-boundary, paragraph.

ordered_steps.title: Build vector store
ordered_steps.description: Embed all chunks and build a persistent FAISS or Chroma index.

ordered_steps.title: Build retrieval + generation
ordered_steps.description: Implement the full RAG pipeline with proper prompt engineering.

ordered_steps.title: Evaluation
ordered_steps.description: Write 10 test questions with expected answers. Score your system.

ordered_steps.title: Documentation
ordered_steps.description: Document the pipeline, chunking decisions, and evaluation results.

practice_suggestions: Compare your RAG answers with pure LLM answers (no retrieval) on your test set

learning_objectives: Add a reranker to an existing RAG pipeline

ordered_steps.title: Install cross-encoder
ordered_steps.description: pip install sentence-transformers[cross-encoders]

ordered_steps.title: Add reranking step
ordered_steps.description: After retrieving top-10, rerank with cross-encoder and keep top-3.

ordered_steps.title: Measure improvement
ordered_steps.description: Compare answer quality on 5 test questions before and after reranking.

practice_suggestions: Measure latency overhead of reranking vs quality gain

learning_objectives: Implement hybrid search and reranking
Measure with RAGAS

ordered_steps.title: Add BM25 index
ordered_steps.description: Install rank_bm25 and build a keyword index alongside your vector index.

ordered_steps.title: Hybrid retrieval
ordered_steps.description: Implement hybrid search: combine BM25 and vector scores with reciprocal rank fusion.

ordered_steps.title: Add reranking
ordered_steps.description: Add a cross-encoder reranker after hybrid retrieval.

ordered_steps.title: RAGAS evaluation
ordered_steps.description: pip install ragas. Measure faithfulness and answer relevance on your test set.

practice_suggestions: Test on questions where exact keyword matching beats semantic search

learning_objectives: Build a production intermediate RAG system with hybrid search, reranking, parent-child chunking, and RAGAS evaluation

ordered_steps.title: Parent-child chunking
ordered_steps.description: Implement parent-child chunking: small chunks (150 tokens) for retrieval, parent chunks (500 tokens) for context.

ordered_steps.title: Hybrid search with BM25
ordered_steps.description: Implement full hybrid retrieval pipeline.

ordered_steps.title: Reranking
ordered_steps.description: Add cross-encoder reranking to hybrid search.

ordered_steps.title: RAGAS evaluation
ordered_steps.description: Set up RAGAS with 20 test Q&A pairs. Measure all available metrics.

ordered_steps.title: Iterate and improve
ordered_steps.description: Based on RAGAS results, identify the weakest metric and improve it.

ordered_steps.title: Document findings
ordered_steps.description: Write a report of your evaluation results and improvement strategy.

practice_suggestions: A/B test: vector-only vs hybrid vs hybrid+rerank on the same test set

learning_objectives: Design an agentic RAG architecture

ordered_steps.title: Read agentic RAG paper
ordered_steps.description: Read the Self-RAG paper abstract and key ideas.

ordered_steps.title: Design architecture
ordered_steps.description: Sketch an agentic RAG flow where the LLM decides whether to retrieve.

practice_suggestions: Identify 3 query types that benefit from agentic vs static RAG

learning_objectives: Implement agentic RAG with dynamic retrieval decisions

ordered_steps.title: Implement retrieval as a tool
ordered_steps.description: Wrap your RAG retrieval as an LLM-callable tool.

ordered_steps.title: Agentic loop
ordered_steps.description: Implement the agentic loop where the LLM decides when to call the retrieval tool.

ordered_steps.title: Multi-hop retrieval
ordered_steps.description: Test a 2-hop query that requires two separate retrievals.

practice_suggestions: Compare cost: static RAG (always retrieves once) vs agentic RAG (decides per query)

learning_objectives: Build a production agentic RAG system with semantic caching, contextual compression, and full evaluation

ordered_steps.title: Agentic RAG implementation
ordered_steps.description: Full implementation with dynamic retrieval decisions and multi-hop support.

ordered_steps.title: Contextual compression
ordered_steps.description: Add a compression step that summarizes retrieved chunks before injection.

ordered_steps.title: Semantic caching
ordered_steps.description: Implement semantic caching with Redis and an invalidation strategy.

ordered_steps.title: Full evaluation
ordered_steps.description: Evaluate: agentic vs static RAG, with/without compression, with/without caching.

ordered_steps.title: Production guide
ordered_steps.description: Write a guide on when to use each RAG variant.

practice_suggestions: Deploy semantic caching and measure cache hit rate on real usage patterns

learning_objectives: Write effective system prompts and few-shot examples

ordered_steps.title: Study the anatomy of a good prompt
ordered_steps.description: Read: role, context, task, format, constraints. Analyze 3 example prompts.

ordered_steps.title: Rewrite a weak prompt
ordered_steps.description: Take a one-sentence prompt and rewrite it with full system prompt + 2 examples.

ordered_steps.title: Test with different temperatures
ordered_steps.description: Run the same prompt at temp=0, 0.5, 1.0. Observe output differences.

practice_suggestions: Write a system prompt for a customer service bot with specific tone and scope constraints

learning_objectives: Master zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought prompting

ordered_steps.title: Zero-shot baseline
ordered_steps.description: Pick 5 tasks and write zero-shot prompts. Test each.

ordered_steps.title: Add few-shot examples
ordered_steps.description: Rewrite each prompt with 3 examples. Compare output quality.

ordered_steps.title: Add chain-of-thought
ordered_steps.description: Add 'Think step by step' to the 2 most complex tasks. Measure accuracy change.

ordered_steps.title: Iterate and document
ordered_steps.description: Document what worked and what did not for each technique.

practice_suggestions: Pick a math word problem and compare zero-shot vs chain-of-thought accuracy

learning_objectives: Build a complete prompt engineering toolkit for a real project

ordered_steps.title: Project selection
ordered_steps.description: Pick a real task (email classification, code review, data extraction). Define success criteria.

ordered_steps.title: Prompt iteration
ordered_steps.description: Write 5 versions of the prompt. Test each on 10 inputs. Score the outputs.

ordered_steps.title: Few-shot selection
ordered_steps.description: Select the best 5 examples for few-shot. Test with and without them.

ordered_steps.title: Format engineering
ordered_steps.description: Optimize the output format (JSON, markdown, plain text). Test each.

ordered_steps.title: Final evaluation
ordered_steps.description: Run your best prompt on 20 test inputs. Compute accuracy/quality score.

ordered_steps.title: Documentation
ordered_steps.description: Document all prompt versions, test results, and the final decision.

practice_suggestions: Share your best prompt with a colleague and ask them to try to break it

learning_objectives: Implement structured output prompting with validation

ordered_steps.title: Write a JSON output prompt
ordered_steps.description: Prompt an LLM to extract entities as JSON. Add schema description in the prompt.

ordered_steps.title: Add validation and retry
ordered_steps.description: Parse the output with json.loads(). On failure, retry with the error message in the prompt.

ordered_steps.title: Test edge cases
ordered_steps.description: Send inputs that are likely to cause malformed JSON. Verify recovery.

practice_suggestions: Try using Pydantic for schema validation instead of manual JSON parsing

learning_objectives: Build a 3-step prompt chain with structured outputs

ordered_steps.title: Design the chain
ordered_steps.description: Define 3 steps: extract → classify → generate. Specify input/output schema for each.

ordered_steps.title: Implement step 1
ordered_steps.description: Extract: pull structured data from unstructured text. Add validation.

ordered_steps.title: Implement steps 2 and 3
ordered_steps.description: Feed step 1 output to step 2, step 2 output to step 3.

ordered_steps.title: Error handling
ordered_steps.description: Handle failures at each step gracefully. Do not let one bad step crash the chain.

practice_suggestions: Add a ReAct-style step where the model can request more information before classifying

learning_objectives: Master structured output prompting, chaining, and tool-use prompting with full test coverage

ordered_steps.title: 5-step prompt chain
ordered_steps.description: Build a 5-step pipeline for a complex document processing task.

ordered_steps.title: Tool-use prompting
ordered_steps.description: Add tool calls at 2 steps in the chain. Implement the ReAct pattern.

ordered_steps.title: Negative examples
ordered_steps.description: Add negative examples to the 2 steps most prone to errors.

ordered_steps.title: Test suite
ordered_steps.description: Write 20 test cases covering all steps and edge cases.

ordered_steps.title: Prompt versioning
ordered_steps.description: Set up a simple Git-based prompt registry and record all prompt versions.

ordered_steps.title: Performance analysis
ordered_steps.description: Measure token usage and latency per step. Identify optimization opportunities.

practice_suggestions: A/B test two versions of your most expensive prompt step to see if cheaper wording works equally well

learning_objectives: Study and defend against prompt injection

ordered_steps.title: Craft injection attacks
ordered_steps.description: Write 5 prompt injection attempts against a system prompt you own.

ordered_steps.title: Add defenses
ordered_steps.description: Add input sanitization, privilege separation, and output validation to resist the attacks.

practice_suggestions: Test your defenses against injection from a red-team partner

learning_objectives: Set up DSPy for automatic prompt optimization

ordered_steps.title: Install and configure DSPy
ordered_steps.description: pip install dspy. Configure with your LLM provider.

ordered_steps.title: Define task signature
ordered_steps.description: Write a DSPy signature for a classification task.

ordered_steps.title: Create labeled dataset
ordered_steps.description: Prepare 20 labeled examples for the optimizer.

ordered_steps.title: Run optimizer
ordered_steps.description: Run BootstrapFewShot optimizer. Compare output quality before and after.

practice_suggestions: Compare DSPy-optimized prompt vs your manually written prompt on the same test set

learning_objectives: Build a production prompt management system with versioning, evals, and DSPy optimization

ordered_steps.title: Prompt registry design
ordered_steps.description: Design a prompt registry with: version, author, created_at, eval_score, status fields.

ordered_steps.title: Automated evals
ordered_steps.description: Write an eval harness that runs on every prompt change. Block deploys that lower the score.

ordered_steps.title: DSPy optimization
ordered_steps.description: Apply DSPy to your top 3 most-used prompts.

ordered_steps.title: Injection defense
ordered_steps.description: Audit all prompts for injection vulnerability. Add defenses.

ordered_steps.title: Documentation
ordered_steps.description: Write a prompt engineering guide for your team.

ordered_steps.title: Presentation
ordered_steps.description: Prepare a 10-minute presentation on your findings.

practice_suggestions: Calculate the ROI of prompt optimization: token savings × cost per token × monthly volume

learning_objectives: Write and run your first async Python function

ordered_steps.title: Read the asyncio basics
ordered_steps.description: Read the Python asyncio documentation introduction. Focus on async def, await, asyncio.run.

ordered_steps.title: Write a hello-async function
ordered_steps.description: Write an async function that waits 1 second and prints a message. Run it with asyncio.run().

ordered_steps.title: Concurrent fetch simulation
ordered_steps.description: Write 3 async functions that each 'fetch' (sleep) for different durations. Run them with asyncio.gather() and measure total time.

practice_suggestions: Compare asyncio.gather() vs sequential await for 3 1-second tasks — the difference should be clear

learning_objectives: Build an async HTTP client that fetches multiple URLs concurrently

ordered_steps.title: Install httpx
ordered_steps.description: pip install httpx. Read the async usage docs.

ordered_steps.title: Sequential fetch
ordered_steps.description: Fetch 5 URLs one at a time with httpx.AsyncClient. Measure time.

ordered_steps.title: Concurrent fetch
ordered_steps.description: Refactor to use asyncio.gather(). Measure time. Compare.

ordered_steps.title: Add error handling
ordered_steps.description: Add try/except around each fetch. Return errors as strings instead of raising.

ordered_steps.title: Add timeouts
ordered_steps.description: Add a 5-second timeout per request. Test with a slow URL.

ordered_steps.title: Results summary
ordered_steps.description: Print: URL, status code, response time, error (if any) for all 5 fetches.

practice_suggestions: Fetch 20 URLs and see how asyncio.gather() scales vs sequential

learning_objectives: Master async basics: gather, error handling, timeouts, and async context managers

ordered_steps.title: Async patterns study
ordered_steps.description: Study: gather, create_task, TimeoutError, async with, async for.

ordered_steps.title: Build async data pipeline
ordered_steps.description: Build a pipeline: fetch URLs, parse JSON, filter results, save to file — all async.

ordered_steps.title: Add async context manager
ordered_steps.description: Wrap the HTTP client in a custom async context manager.

ordered_steps.title: Add async generator
ordered_steps.description: Refactor to yield results as they arrive instead of collecting all at once.

ordered_steps.title: Tests
ordered_steps.description: Write pytest-asyncio tests for all components.

ordered_steps.title: Benchmark
ordered_steps.description: Compare async vs sync version on 50 requests. Document results.

practice_suggestions: Replace asyncio.gather() with asyncio.TaskGroup (Python 3.11+) and compare error handling

learning_objectives: Implement asyncio.Queue for producer-consumer pattern

ordered_steps.title: Queue basics
ordered_steps.description: Read asyncio.Queue docs. Understand put, get, join, task_done.

ordered_steps.title: Implement producer-consumer
ordered_steps.description: Build a producer that puts 10 items into a queue and 3 consumers that process them.

practice_suggestions: Add backpressure: limit the queue to 5 items and observe producer blocking

learning_objectives: Build a complete async pipeline with queues, timeouts, and cancellation

ordered_steps.title: Design the pipeline
ordered_steps.description: Define: 1 producer, asyncio.Queue, 3 consumers, 1 result collector.

ordered_steps.title: Implement and run
ordered_steps.description: Build the full pipeline with real async IO operations.

ordered_steps.title: Add graceful shutdown
ordered_steps.description: Handle Ctrl+C: stop producer, wait for queue to empty, cancel consumers.

ordered_steps.title: Add timeouts
ordered_steps.description: Add per-task timeout. Dead tasks go to a dead-letter queue.

practice_suggestions: Benchmark: 3 consumers vs 10 consumers for 100-item queue

learning_objectives: Build a production async service with queues, cancellation, backpressure, and metrics

ordered_steps.title: Service design
ordered_steps.description: Design a multi-stage async processing pipeline with error handling at each stage.

ordered_steps.title: Implement all stages
ordered_steps.description: Build: ingestion, processing, enrichment, output stages.

ordered_steps.title: Backpressure and rate limiting
ordered_steps.description: Add queue size limits and consumer rate limiting.

ordered_steps.title: Graceful shutdown
ordered_steps.description: Implement SIGTERM handling with clean pipeline drain.

ordered_steps.title: Metrics
ordered_steps.description: Add: items/sec, queue depth, error rate, avg processing time per stage.

ordered_steps.title: Load test
ordered_steps.description: Load test at 3 throughput levels. Find the saturation point.

practice_suggestions: Add a circuit breaker that pauses the pipeline if error rate exceeds 10%

learning_objectives: Profile an async application for performance bottlenecks

ordered_steps.title: Enable asyncio debug mode
ordered_steps.description: Set PYTHONASYNCIODEBUG=1. Run your async app. Read the debug output.

ordered_steps.title: Find slow callbacks
ordered_steps.description: Identify callbacks that take >100ms. These are blocking the event loop.

ordered_steps.title: Fix one bottleneck
ordered_steps.description: Move one blocking call to run_in_executor() and measure the improvement.

practice_suggestions: Add asyncio.coroutine profiling with viztracer or py-spy

learning_objectives: Implement uvloop and structured concurrency

ordered_steps.title: Add uvloop
ordered_steps.description: pip install uvloop. Add uvloop.install() before asyncio.run(). Benchmark before/after.

ordered_steps.title: Structured concurrency with anyio
ordered_steps.description: Refactor raw create_task() calls to use anyio TaskGroup.

ordered_steps.title: AsyncExitStack
ordered_steps.description: Use AsyncExitStack to manage 5 async resources that are conditionally opened.

practice_suggestions: Compare error propagation behavior: raw gather() vs anyio TaskGroup when one task fails

learning_objectives: Build a high-performance async service using uvloop, structured concurrency, and comprehensive profiling

ordered_steps.title: Baseline profiling
ordered_steps.description: Profile your current async service. Document all bottlenecks.

ordered_steps.title: Apply uvloop
ordered_steps.description: Add uvloop. Re-benchmark. Document improvement.

ordered_steps.title: Structured concurrency refactor
ordered_steps.description: Refactor all task management to use anyio TaskGroup.

ordered_steps.title: CPU-bound optimization
ordered_steps.description: Move all CPU-bound work to ProcessPoolExecutor.

ordered_steps.title: Final load test
ordered_steps.description: Load test the optimized service. Document gains.

ordered_steps.title: Production guide
ordered_steps.description: Write the async Python production guide for your team.

practice_suggestions: Deploy with Kubernetes HPA and test horizontal scaling of your async service
